context.answer_mapping_notes: pages `1-2` of the answers file are the `Systems Thematic Test 1 Chapter 1: Plant System` answer section

summary.overall_assessment: Emma shows a workable understanding of the plant system topic, with stronger performance on roots, water absorption, and fair-test reasoning than on plant classification and some adaptation questions. The main gaps were in classifying plants by stem type or habitat cues, identifying leaf parts / vein pattern, distinguishing plant-part functions in water plants and leaf-size questions, and the final stem-cutting experiment.

skill_tags: roots absorb water and mineral salts

functions of roots, leaves, and flowers

question_results.student_answer: because the leaf cannot take any more sunlight and cannot make food for the plant.
question_results.correct_answer: The black plastic bags prevented the leaves from receiving light. The plant could not make food and died.

question_results.correct_answer: Strong stems: Bougainvillea shrub, Rain tree; Weak stems: Morning glory plant

question_results.correct_answer: Morning glory can climb up the tree trunks / along the hill to get more sunlight.

question_results.student_answer: Y, Z, X
question_results.correct_answer: Y, Z, X
skill_tags: infer water absorbed from remaining water

question_results.student_answer: Y has no roots and cannot absorb water; Z has many roots so it absorbs almost all the water; X has some roots so it absorbs less than Z but more than Y.
question_results.correct_answer: Z has the most roots and absorbed the most water; Y has the least roots and absorbed the least water; X is in between.

question_results.student_answer: The more roots a plant has, the more water it will absorb.
question_results.correct_answer: The greater the number of roots, the greater the amount of water absorbed by the plant.
skill_tags: relationship between number of roots and water absorption

question_results.student_answer: No, because ... this experiment has two [changed variables], the number of leaves and the amount of roots.
question_results.correct_answer: No. The number of leaves is not the same as in set-ups X and Z. A fair test should have only one changed variable, which should be the number of roots.

question_results.student_answer: P: F, Q: E
question_results.correct_answer: P: F, Q: E

question_results.student_answer: the more leaves, the more water absorbed by the plant.
question_results.correct_answer: The greater the number of leaves, the greater the amount of water absorbed by the plant.

question_results.correct_answer: The plant did not absorb water. The plant may have been artificial / dead.
